ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS AND MODIFIES ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION: SECOND AND THIRD AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION: SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH AND FIFTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION: SECOND, THIRD, FOURTH, FIFTH AND SIXTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION: SECOND, THIRD AND FOURTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION: SECOND, FOURTH, FIFTH, SIXTH AND SEVENTH AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES. EXCEPT FOR THE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BASED ON FAILURE TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION, THE BALANCE OF THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. JUDGE: PATRICK J. Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Request For Admissions, And Form Interrogatories, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-21-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED.
Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, March 21, 2007. Plaintiff Barry White's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories, request for production of documents and requests for admissions.
Request for production
Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken.
3, 4, 9:
As defendant initially stated she did not produce any documents, defendant is ordered to identify the documents produced in response to each category.
6, 7, 11:
As defendant initially stated that any documents were equally available to plaintiff, and now states either documents have been produced or will be produced, defendant is ordered to identify the documents produced in response to each category.

Requests for admissions
The objections are overruled and stricken. The amended responses, as well as the qualifications pursuant to CCP 2033.220 (b)(1) in requests #2, 5, and 6, are otherwise sufficient.

Form interrogatories
15.1
Granted. Defendant is ordered to respond fully.
17.1
Granted. Defendant is ordered to respond fully as to the denials of requests for admissions 2, 3, 8, 10, and 16

Where the Court has ordered further answers, defendant shall serve responses within 15 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that defendant asserted unmeritorious objections and made evasive responses. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,090 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
